summary: Large `un/` decomp organization pass that split the monolithic `src/melee/un/un_2FC9.c` object into eight smaller objects `un_2FC91.c` through `un_2FC98.c`, updated GALE01 split ranges and symbols, moved/refined `DevText` and name-tag related declarations, and touched nearby gm/lb/gr/ty code mostly for include, prototype, and formatting fallout. The decomp.dev report showed net matched-code progress (+6128 bytes, 117 new matches), but the author noted three real regressions: `un_802FE260` fuzzy match dropped 72.73% to 59.74%, `DevText_Setup` dropped 76.32% to 10.14% due to unwanted inlining likely fixable with better text splits, and `fn_803020F8` was deleted because it was not a real function.
what_changed: Deleted the single 1912-line `src/melee/un/un_2FC9.c` source file and deleted `src/melee/un/un_2FC9.static.h`, replacing them with eight slice files `un_2FC91.c` through `un_2FC98.c`.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so the old `melee/un/un_2FC9.c` text/data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges are partitioned across `un_2FC91.c` through `un_2FC98.c`, preserving address-contiguous text ranges from `0x802FC9B4` through `0x80305058`.
Updated `configure.py` to build the new `un` object list; `un_2FC92.c` is marked `Matching`, while the other seven new `un_2FC9x.c` objects are marked `NonMatching`.
Changed release compiler flags in `configure.py` from only `-DNDEBUG=1` to `-sym off -DNDEBUG=1`.

Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`: removed `fn_803020F8`, expanded `DevText_InitPool` from size `0x13C` to `0x140`, corrected several data/sdata/sbss object sizes, split `un_803F9B30`, and renamed local jump tables from `jumptable_803FDCA8` style to `jtbl_803FDCA8` style.

Refined many prototypes in `src/melee/un/un_2FC9.h`, especially around `DevText` and `un_80302DF0` through `un_80304D30`; examples include `DevText_Create(char, int, int, int, int, char*)`, `un_80302DF0` returning `struct un_80304138_objalloc_t*`, and several previously `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` functions becoming typed `int`, `bool`, `void`, or `HSD_GObj*` declarations.

Moved/added `un_80304138_objalloc_t` and nested `un_80304138_objalloc_t_x8` layout definitions into `src/melee/un/types.h` with `STATIC_ASSERT` size checks, while removing older placeholder structs `un_80302DF8_t` and `un_80304334_t`.
Removed the forward declaration for `un_80304334_t` from `src/melee/un/forward.h` because the header now uses the fuller objalloc struct type.

Added a real guarded `src/melee/ty/tylist.h` header with declarations for `un_803124BC` and `un_803127D4`, and adjusted users to include `ty/tylist.h` instead of relying on `un_2FC9.h` for those toy/list functions.

Touched `gm_16AE.c`, `gm_1A4C.c`, `gmmain_lib.c`, `lbcardnew.c`, `gryorster.c`, `ground.c`, `lbcardgame.c`, and toy headers mostly for include-order cleanup, line wrapping, function prototype refinements such as `void` parameter lists, and downstream typing fallout from the `un/` split.
smart_moves: The monolithic unknown object was not just copied into several files; the PR also updated splits, build object registration, symbols, headers, and downstream include dependencies together, which is necessary for object-split decomp work to remain buildable and searchable.

The split boundaries were encoded in `splits.txt` across text and data sections, making future matching work more localized: `un_2FC97` contains the `DevText_StrLen` through `DevText_Draw` region and `un_2FC98` contains later `DevText`/unknown UI utility code.
Deleting `fn_803020F8` and extending `DevText_InitPool` by four bytes corrected a bogus function boundary rather than preserving a misleading symbol for match-count appearance.
Replacing placeholder prototypes in `un_2FC9.h` with concrete signatures and concrete struct pointer types gives future decomp work better type pressure and fewer `UNK_PARAMS` callsites.

Moving object-allocation struct definitions into `un/types.h` with explicit `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x20)` documents ABI-sensitive layout facts and reduces reliance on local static-header placeholders.
Adding `-sym off` for non-debug builds is a build-configuration detail that can affect generated output and should be kept in mind when matching compiler output.

The author explicitly separated noisy split-induced report churn from actual regressions, identifying that many broken matches were caused by moving functions out of the old `un_2FC9` unit rather than real losses.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large unknown object, expect decomp.dev to report many old-unit broken matches and new-unit matches; compare by function identity and new units before treating the report as a true regression.
Text split boundaries can influence inlining. The author specifically noted that `DevText_Setup` was inlining something it should not and suggested better text splits as the likely fix.

Do not keep four-byte or artificial function symbols solely because they matched before; if evidence shows the bytes belong to the prior function, delete the bogus symbol and adjust the real function size.

Splitting source objects requires synchronized updates to `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, `configure.py`, source includes, headers, and forward declarations. Missing any one of these can create false regressions or dependency leakage.
Replacing `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` with typed signatures is valuable even in still-nonmatching files because it improves downstream decompilation and helps remove placeholder structs.
Use dedicated subsystem headers such as `ty/tylist.h` for declarations that belong outside the split source, instead of letting unrelated headers like `un_2FC9.h` accumulate cross-system prototypes.
For ABI-sensitive decomp struct work, pair new layout hypotheses with `STATIC_ASSERT` size checks so future edits fail loudly if layout assumptions drift.
naming_conventions: New split files follow the address-derived `un_2FC91.c` through `un_2FC98.c` naming pattern, extending the original `un_2FC9` prefix rather than inventing semantic names prematurely.
Local jump-table symbols were normalized from `jumptable_803FDCA8` to shorter `jtbl_803FDCA8`, `jtbl_803FDCCC`, and `jtbl_803FDCF0`.
Known debug text functions use the `DevText_` prefix, such as `DevText_InitPool`, `DevText_SetupCObj`, `DevText_Create`, `DevText_Print`, `DevText_Printf`, and `DevText_EraseFirstLine`.
Address-based unknown functions remain in `un_802...` / `un_803...` form when no semantic name is evidenced, while compiler-helper/internal uncertain functions keep `fn_...` names.
Struct names for still-unknown layouts use address-derived names with context suffixes, e.g. `struct un_80304138_objalloc_t` and `struct un_80304138_objalloc_t_x8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Object split ranges in `splits.txt` were used as a matching tactic to isolate smaller chunks of the old `un_2FC9.c` address range, with `un_2FC92.c` immediately marked `Matching` in `configure.py`.
`DevText_InitPool` was resized from `0x13C` to `0x140` and `fn_803020F8` was removed, indicating a function-boundary correction at `0x803020F8`.
The PR corrected data object sizes and split data objects, e.g. `un_803F9B30` from `0x2D8` to `0x218` plus new `un_803F9D48` and `lbl_803F9D84`, to better align data with code references.
Release builds now include `-sym off`, which is relevant when comparing generated object output against retail matching expectations.
Better text split selection was identified as the likely tactic to stop unwanted `DevText_Setup` inlining.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported `Total functions: 19890 (-1)`, `Matched code: 36.80% (+0.16%, +6128 bytes)`, and `Linked code: 24.66% (+0.02%, +780 bytes)`.

decomp.dev listed 117 new matches, including `NameTag_Create`, `fn_802FCC44`, `un_802FD708`, `DevText_SetupCObj`, `DevText_InitPool`, `DevText_NumToStr`, and many `un_80302...`/`un_80304...` functions in the new split units.
The author clarified actual regressions as `un_802FE260` fuzzy match `72.73% -> 59.74%`, `DevText_Setup` fuzzy match `76.32% -> 10.14%`, and `fn_803020F8` `100.00% -> 0.00%`.

The author stated they were not sure what happened to `un_802FE260`, that `DevText_Setup` is inlining something it should not and should probably be fixed with better text splits, and that `fn_803020F8` was not a real function and was deleted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fix `DevText_Setup` inlining or adjust `un_2FC97`/`un_2FC98` text split boundaries.
Search for later changes to `un_802FE260` after this split, since the author did not know why its fuzzy match regressed.
Check whether more `un_2FC9x` objects are later promoted from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, especially `un_2FC91`, `un_2FC93`, `un_2FC97`, and `un_2FC98`.
Search for additional cleanup of `un_80304138_objalloc_t` field names once the object allocation/UI utility code becomes better understood.
Search for repository-wide use of the `jtbl_` naming convention replacing `jumptable_`.
